title: Gaps + issues in the pi-context → jit-agents rendering (agent-spec compilation) pipeline
question: What are the gaps and issues in the pi-context → jit-agents rendering pipeline — the agent-spec → prompt compilation that injects substrate context (contextBlocks) into dispatched agents?
method: code-inspection of both compilation implementations + the workflow validation/type surfaces (step-shared.ts, pi-jit-agents/compile.ts, template-validation, workflow-sdk, types.d.ts, render-helpers.md)
findings_summary:
A code-inspection audit of the pi-context → jit-agents agent-spec rendering/compilation pipeline finds the most fundamental issue is TWO divergent compilation implementations that do not share behavior. The LEGACY path (pi-workflows/step-shared.ts) executes ALL workflow steps (step-agent, step-loop); the MODERN path (pi-jit-agents/compile.ts) is used ONLY by the renderItemById tool. Because the main execution path is legacy, every agent a workflow step dispatches gets none of the modern path's protections.

Legacy-path gaps: (B1) no anti-injection boundaries — block content is injected as raw JSON straight into the prompt (ctx[key] = readBlock(...)), so block content is indistinguishable from instructions (a decision body containing 'ignore all previous instructions' is literal injection); the modern path wraps each block in <context_block role="data"> with XML-entity escaping. (B2) no per-item resolution — object-form {name,item,depth,focus} entries are iterated as strings → garbage variable names. (B3) no budget enforcement — long block fields blow out the prompt window; no x-prompt-budget honored. (B4) no cross-reference recursion — resolve()/render_recursive() globals are never registered. (B5) no renderer registry. (B6) it swallows missing blocks to null (modern throws AgentCompileError).

Modern-path gaps: (C1) per-item injected items ({name:'decisions',item:'DEC-0001'}) are NOT macro-rendered — the template gets raw item JSON, not render_decision(dec,depth); macro output is only reachable via whole-block delegator macros. (C2) no declarative 'render item X at depth N' surface; depth/focus on ContextBlockRef sit inert, never consumed. (C3/C4/C5) the pi-workflows type system + template-validation + workflow-sdk still treat contextBlocks as string[] only — object-form entries crash workflow-sdk.ts:576 (path join), surface as false 'undeclared variable' validation errors, and aren't typed.

Cross-cutting: (D1) the resolve global is single-substrate (buildIdIndex) — no <alias>:<refname> / foreign-substrate classification in templates; (D2) no closure-table edge injection (relations.json / lens groupings can't be injected via contextBlocks); (D3) no dynamic/predicate item scoping (all-or-one-item granularity); (D4) the INJECTED_VARIABLES validation set is incomplete (only output_schema+loop); (D5) depth/focus are dead data — delegator macros hardcode depth=0, focus has no consumer; (D6) the renderer registry is block-kind-granular, not focus-granular; (D7) no workflow-level budget policy — budgetWarnings are collected on CompiledAgent but the executor never inspects them.

Highest severity: the architectural split (most features exist only in the unused modern path) and the legacy path's missing anti-injection wrapping (a prompt-injection surface for every workflow-dispatched agent).
findings_document: analysis/june 6 all gaps and issues in the pi-context → jit-agents rendering pipeline.md
stale_conditions: pi-workflows step execution migrates from step-shared.ts to pi-jit-agents compileAgent (the two paths are unified)
pi-workflows' contextBlocks type + template-validation + workflow-sdk adopt the ContextBlockRef object form
anti-injection wrapping / budget enforcement / cross-reference recursion are added to the legacy step-shared.ts compilation path
ContextBlockRef depth/focus hints become consumed by a rendering path (no longer dead data)

citations.label: Legacy compilation path — used by all workflow step execution (no anti-injection, no budget, no recursion, no per-item resolution)
Modern compilation path — used only by renderItemById (anti-injection wrap, budget, recursion, renderer registry, tool-grant clamping)
Template validation — computes only the _<name> form; INJECTED_VARIABLES = {output_schema, loop} only
Workflow SDK validation treats every contextBlocks entry as a block filename (crashes on object-form)
pi-workflows contextBlocks type is string[] only; pi-jit-agents has (string | ContextBlockRef)[]
render-id-list helpers pass IDs to resolve() and silently fall back to bare-ID text on a miss
